title: Bakery Style Zucchini Muffins
description: Extra-large, soft, moist muffins, just like the ones you'd find in your favorite bakery display case. Made with fresh shredded zucchini and simple pantry ingredients, these muffins are perfectly sweet and fluffy.

data.summary: Extra-large, soft, moist muffins, just like the ones you'd find in your favorite bakery display case. Made with fresh shredded zucchini and simple pantry ingredients, these muffins are perfectly sweet and fluffy.

items: 2 1/4 cups all-purpose gluten free flour (I recommend King Arthur's)

steps: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit

Line a 12-cup muffin tin with 6 parchment paper liners, leaving an empty space between each muffin cup. To make the liners, cut parchment paper into squares and make a slit on all four sides toward the center. This allows the muffins to rise tall and develop their signature bakery-style shape without spilling over the sides.
In a large mixing bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
In a separate mixing bowl, whisk together the eggs, oil, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and vanilla extract until smooth and well combined.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until thoroughly combined.
Fold in the shredded zucchini and chocolate chips until evenly distributed throughout the batter.
7. Using a cookie scoop, fill each muffin liner about 90% full. I used a tablespoon-sized scoop and added 4 scoops of batter to each liner.

8. Bake at 425 degrees Fahrenheit for 7 minutes. Without opening the oven door, reduce the temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for 12- 18 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
9. Remove the muffins from the pan and transfer them to a wire rack to cool completely.
